publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 222-223. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: I regret to find that such serious preparations for defence are quietly making by government as look as if they really expected war! This much disturbs me. War is a fearful thing, in many points of view.
All likely men have been registered this summer for enlistment, & are ordered to [two words crossed out] keep their certificate (with description
of their person) bye them. I saw the certificates of my boatmen.--
The Martello towers have also been repaired; and I find all the lower classes expect war at any moment, if Louis-Philippe dies.
How is it that the yeomanry are not ordered to meet & practise in the different Counties? I should have expected this would be an early
I suppose that you would endeavour of course to take a part, if war does break out. It is your vocation; you understand it, & a brave & gallant officer & soldier you would make!--
Did you get my few lines from Seaford, of Sunday?--
